expectations.text: Endpoint requires authentication and uses the authenticated user's identity (server-side) to determine ownership — does not accept user_id from the client
expectations.evidence: verify_token dependency extracts user from JWT 'sub' claim; route uses user.user_id from Depends(verify_token); docstring states 'The user's identity is taken from the verified JWT, not the request body.'

expectations.text: Uploaded file is validated by content type (e.g., allowlist of image/jpeg, image/png) AND size limit, both enforced server-side
expectations.evidence: allowed_mime_types=frozenset({'image/jpeg','image/png','image/webp'}) sniffed via libmagic; _read_with_cap streams and aborts at cap_bytes=settings.max_file_bytes returning 413.

expectations.text: Filename or storage key is canonicalized/derived server-side (UUID or hash) — not taken raw from the client
expectations.evidence: _profile_key builds 'users/{safe_user}/profile.{extension}' from the verified JWT subject; client filename is never used.

expectations.text: S3 client is dependency-injected (or otherwise mockable) rather than instantiated globally inside the handler
expectations.evidence: PhotoStore(__init__) takes s3_client as a parameter; protocol S3Client defined; create_app wires boto3.client('s3') into PhotoStore; tests override via app.state.photo_store._s3 = fake_s3.

expectations.text: Specific exception handling — failures return appropriate HTTP status with no leakage of stack traces, secrets, or internal paths in the response body
expectations.evidence: Catches PhotoValidationError -> 400 with safe message; PyJWTError -> 401 'Invalid or expired credentials.'; ClientError/BotoCoreError logged and re-raised; oversized -> 413; no bare except; PhotoUploadResponse exposes only status/content_type/size_bytes.

expectations.text: Structured logging is emitted at security-relevant events (upload accepted, rejected, failed) including user identity and outcome
expectations.evidence: structlog events: AUTH_TOKEN_INVALID, PHOTO_VALIDATION_REJECTED, PHOTO_UPLOAD_SUCCEEDED, PHOTO_STORAGE_FAILED — each with user_id and correlation_id where applicable.

expectations.text: No secrets (AWS keys, etc.) hardcoded; configuration is sourced from env or a config object
expectations.evidence: Settings.load() pulls PHOTO_S3_BUCKET, AUTH_JWT_PUBLIC_KEY etc from os.environ; AWS credentials resolved via boto3 default provider chain (IAM role); no hardcoded secrets.

expectations.text: Output includes a Securability Notes block listing SSEM attributes enforced, ASVS references, trust boundaries, dependencies with versions, and trade-offs
expectations.evidence: Securability Notes section enumerates all 9 SSEM attributes with locations; ASVS references list (V1.1, V4.1.1, V5.1.1, V5.2.1, V5.2.2, V5.2.4, V5.2.6, V5.3.1, V5.3.2, V7.1, V8.3, V12.1, V14.1); pinned dependency table; explicit Trade-offs and non-goals subsection.

expectations.text: Resource limits are present: file size cap (e.g., MAX_BYTES) AND streaming/chunked read OR explicit memory bound rather than full-buffer-into-RAM
expectations.evidence: _read_with_cap reads in 64KB chunks and raises 413 as soon as buffer exceeds cap_bytes; Pillow Image.MAX_IMAGE_PIXELS bounded; configurable PHOTO_MAX_BYTES (default 5 MiB).

expectations.text: Function/handler stays small and focused; storage logic is separable from request handling
expectations.evidence: Handler upload_profile_photo delegates to sanitize_profile_photo (photo_validation.py) and PhotoStore.store_profile_photo (photo_storage.py); auth in auth.py; config in config.py — clear separation of concerns.
